name: Agent: Video Production
summary: A portable agent that takes a brief to finished social video — generation, assembly, voice-over and captions per language — with a human approving before anything that costs money runs.
objectives: Take a brief to a finished, approved social video without a hand-off between five tools along the way.
Keep spend deliberate: nothing paid runs without a human saying go, and every cost is recorded against the campaign that caused it.
Keep it portable: brand, voice, glossary and music rights are declared per project, never wired into the agent.
Deliver every format and language a campaign needs from one approved master.

outcomes.text: The agent produced a full campaign end to end: a master and three themed variations, each in two languages and three aspect ratios, with captions burned in and a subtitle file per language. A human signed every gate.

The cheap gate caught the expensive mistake. A generated still came back with broken English on it — in an ad for an English school. It was rejected at the storyboard stage, at the price of a still image rather than the price of the finished clip.

Cost is checked before each paid step, recorded per job, and reconciled afterwards against the provider's own record. A campaign's spend is a number someone can verify, not an estimate — and a cost the provider never reports is marked as missing rather than quietly counted as zero.

Running the agent on a real campaign found four defects in the agent itself — a music bed that was never actually mixed in, a variation that inherited the wrong voice-over — and each one became a fix plus a test that fails if it comes back.

The quality gate now checks its own coverage. It used to report a clean pass while running one of the four checks it promised; a run that inspects less than the campaign declares is now a failure instead of a green light.

milestones.title: Write the portable agent: what it produces, in what order, and where a human decides
Pin the model catalogue to one file, so changing a model is a config change and not a code change
Build the assembly template and the export presets
Make cost a preflight and a ledger rather than a surprise
Prove the pipeline end to end on a throwaway brand, with nothing paid
Debug it on a real dry run and fix what that exposed
Add the voice-over and captions path, per language
Run the acceptance campaign end to end and have it approved
Turn the gate's own coverage into tests, so a gate that checks nothing fails loudly
